Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted. Plaintiffs' responses violate the requirements that the responses be 'straightforward' and 'not evasive As to RFAs 1 and 2, if, as it appears, plaintiffs contend that they lack sufficient information to admit or deny that Mr. Reents was exposed to amphibole fibers from thermal insulation, plaintiffs should so state without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFAs 3 and 4, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents inhaled amphibole fibers and such inhalation was a substantial factor in causing his mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFA 13, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents was exposed to asbestos-containing products for which defendant is not liable, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFA 31, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that asbestos fibers from products attributable to Johns Manville were a substantial factor in causing Mr. Reents' mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. Plaintiffs' purported 'qualifications' in their responses are much more in the nature of obfuscations than proper qualifications. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
